title: Pre-Approval Checklist Config
description: One file per form category. Adding a new category means adding a new file matching this schema — the orchestration engine reads this data, it never hard-codes category logic.

appeal_of.description: category_id of the base category this appeal re-runs checks against (Appeals only).

form_template_signature.description: Distinctive strings expected in the PDF text layer that identify this form type.

item_name_field.description: field id whose value names the specific class/item/membership/program being requested — used generically by fee_match and exclusion_list checks so the evaluator never hard-codes a field name per category.

fee_field.description: field id holding the fee/price stated on the form, used generically by fee_match checks.

fields.description: Header fields to extract from the form.

verifiable.description: web = tool checks it; internal = human/back-office only, never guessed; document = needs an uploaded letter/attachment, not the website.

check_type.description: rule = deterministic code path; llm_judgment = grounded LLM call over captured page text, still schema-validated; fee_match = numeric comparison with tolerance; exclusion_list = deterministic keyword/category match against an exclusion list.

evidence_label.description: Label burned into the targeted evidence screenshot, e.g. 'Evidence: published fees'.

caps.description: Numeric thresholds referenced by fee_match checks, e.g. {"per_session": 111, "per_year": 500}.

explanation.description: Criterion-specific, plain-language reason an internal/document criterion can't be checked from the website (e.g. 'Requires the participant's approved budget record'). Shown instead of a generic boilerplate line.

form_question.description: Verbatim question text as it appears on the PDF form's YES/NO checklist table, used to locate and parse the applicant's own checked answer. Defaults to `question` if omitted; set explicitly whenever `question` has been reworded/annotated for report readability (e.g. a '(must be NO)' suffix added for clarity) so the verbatim match still succeeds.

group.description: Display grouping for internal criteria, e.g. 'Budget and funding', 'Participant plan and outcomes', 'Provider and programme eligibility', 'Duplication of services', 'Reimbursement and invoicing'.
